facts_disclosed: Thinking about moving to New Jersey near Naomi — undecided

turns.content: hey so i got something on my mind and i cant figure it out no matter how many miles i drive thinking about it
I'm all ears. What's been weighing on you?

okay so you know how i live in atlanta right i got my little studio apartment here and my cousin marc is in decatur and theres this whole haitian community out there that i been part of for years like those are my people you know we do the sunday cookouts and the dominoes and all that and its cheap to live here compared to a lot of places
Sounds like you've got a good setup — community, family nearby, affordable. What's making you reconsider?

naomi man its always about naomi so shes up in newark with her mom and i see her when i can but even with the northeast routes its not enough like i was thinking what if i just moved up there near newark so on my days off i could actually be around her you know take her to school pick her up go to her games all that stuff that dads are supposed to do instead of being 800 miles away eating at a pilot

That makes total sense. Being close enough to do the everyday dad stuff — school pickup, weekend time — that's different from the occasional visit when your route lines up. Have you looked into what the cost of living difference would be?

thats the thing man jersey is expensive like crazy expensive i looked at apartments near newark and for what i pay in atlanta for my studio id be living in somebody's basement up there for real and i been looking at some local driving jobs because im tired of over the road right but those jobs are mostly here in atlanta not up in jersey so if i move i gotta find work up there too and i dont know anybody in the trucking world up north

That's a real practical barrier. Atlanta you've got your apartment, Marc, the community, and the job leads. Jersey would be starting fresh in a more expensive market. Have you talked to Danielle about it — like would she be on board with you being around more?

danielle would be fine with it honestly she always said naomi needs her dad more and shes right about that i know shes right but then i think about leaving decatur and all those people there and marc is like a brother to me you know we came up together from miami and he is the only family i got in this state and the haitian spot on memorial drive where we go on saturdays man i cant imagine not having that

It sounds like you're weighing two things that both matter deeply — your daughter versus your community and support system. Both are valid and important. It doesn't have to be one is right and the other is wrong.

exactly thats what makes it so hard because some nights im driving through virginia and im like why am i not closer to my kid this is stupid just move and then i get back to atlanta and go to marcs house and eat real food and talk creole with people who get me and im like nah this is home right here sa k pase i go back and forth every single week

That back and forth makes sense — you're feeling the pull from both sides depending on where you are in the moment. Have you thought about giving yourself a deadline to decide, or is this more of a long-term thing you're sitting with?

i dont know man i think right now im just sitting with it you know because making a move like that when youre not sure is how you end up regretting everything and i dont want to be in some apartment in jersey by myself with no community missing atlanta just as much as i miss naomi right now like i could trade one loneliness for another and that dont make sense

That's actually really wise — recognizing that the move could just shift the loneliness instead of solving it. You'd be closer to Naomi but you'd lose the support network that keeps you grounded. There's no wrong answer here and you don't have to rush it.

yeah i think for now im staying put in atlanta and just trying to see her more when i can maybe if i get one of those local jobs here i can fly up to newark on my days off or something i dont know bondye bon its a lot to figure out but thanks for letting me talk through it because out here on the road you just think in circles and never get anywhere with it you know

Sometimes talking it through is what you need even if you don't land on an answer right away. Staying in Atlanta for now while you figure out the local job situation makes sense — you don't have to uproot everything to be a great dad. And hey, the fact that you're thinking this hard about it says a lot about how much Naomi means to you. You'll figure it out.
